page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7nex.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz, with 2 physical cores and 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1880.59 and a gzip compression rate of 27.64 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory performance metrics are generally lower, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1931.67 and a PassMark CPU score of 2968.21. Memory bandwidth is also limited, measuring 14.51 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 13.41 GB/sec for write operations.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource ratio, this instance provides a stable entry-level environment for workloads that do not require high-performance multi-core processing or local storage. It is suitable for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, and small-scale development or testing environments.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7nex.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core, and operates on the KVM hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, such as a stress-ng single-core score of 1880.59, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively low. This hardware profile makes the instance suitable for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and development environments that require dedicated CPU resources without massive memory or storage demands.
